name: ambari
explanation: Ambari is the Hadoop cluster management REST API (typically on port 8080). The check walks the `/api/v1/clusters/...` tree to enumerate hosts, services, and components. Although `username`/`password` are marked optional in the spec, every production Ambari server has authentication enabled (default credentials are notoriously `admin:admin`, which a generic auto-config layer must not assume), and even discovering which `services` and `components` to scrape requires user policy: the `services` mapping is a per-deployment whitelist of metric headers per component. Per pattern D (credential-required REST API) and the user-intent caveat, this is `impossible`.
notes: URL field is just `localhost` in the README example, but real deployments need scheme+host+port plus basic-auth credentials. The `services` whitelist is also a user-policy input that auto-config cannot synthesize.
